Return the cross-asset correlation card for a Polymarket slug.

Computes Pearson correlation of the market's logit-prob innovations
against the log-return (or yield-difference) innovations of seven
cross-asset benchmarks: BTC-USD, ETH-USD, SPY, GLD, DXY (UUP proxy),
VIX (^VIX), and DGS10. For each benchmark, also reports the lag in

Returns:

get.description:
Open a Server-Sent Events stream of live midpoints for ``slugs``.

The stream emits one ``tick`` event per slug per cycle, sleeps ``1/hz``
seconds, and self-terminates after :data:`MAX_STREAM_SECONDS` so the
client reconnects (a standard SSE pattern that survives proxy idle
timeouts and prevents server-side memory leaks on long sessions).

Cointegrated-peer lookup for a factor slug.

Searches the alpha-hunter sweep cache for every pair where ``slug``
is one of the two legs and returns the top ``top`` peers by
out-of-sample Sharpe (filtered by ``min_sharpe``). Each peer is
annotated with display name and theme from ``factors.yml`` and a
tier classification from the curated strategies file.

When the alpha-hunter sweep cache is empty (or missing on disk), the
endpoint degrades gracefully: it returns 200 with ``degraded_mode=true``
and a basic theme-matched peer list derived from ``factors.yml`` so the
UI panel still has something to render. ``reason`` carries the cause
(``alpha_hunter_cache_unavailable``). An empty peer list with
``n_peers == 0`` and ``degraded_mode == false`` is also a valid response
— it means the slug has no cointegrated counterparts in a populated
sweep set.

Return every currently-actionable calendar arb across curated clusters.

A signal is *actionable* when the absolute log λ-ratio between the two
legs equals or exceeds 0.75 — the threshold at which Strategy-28's
revalidation cell shows a positive net Sharpe (+1.78) after the 3.6 %
round-trip taker cost.

Sorted by |log λ-ratio| descending so the highest-conviction signals
appear first. Empty list when no cluster crosses the bar.

schema.title: Response Get Active Signals Terminal Calendar Scanner Active Get

get.description:
90-day cluster-level PnL backtest of the threshold-crossing signal.

For each day in the window, recomputes the pairwise log λ-ratio and,
when it crosses the 0.75 threshold while the book is flat, enters a
5-day-hold position in the direction implied by the sign of the
ratio. PnL is Δlog(price) per leg, netted long − short, with half
the round-trip cost charged on entry.

Returns an empty point list (and zero stats) when live price history
cannot be fetched — the endpoint never 500s on a data-source outage.

List **only** the currently-actionable tickets across every cluster.

Returns the subset whose action is ``OPEN_PAIR`` — so the user can
open the Terminal and see "your move right now: …" without having

get.summary: GDELT news events with Polymarket price-reaction windows.
get.description:
Return per-event price reactions (1h/6h/24h) for a Polymarket slug.

Pipeline:
  1. Resolve the market metadata via Gamma (cached upstream).
  2. Hit GDELT 2.0 + Polymarket hourly-prices in parallel — the two
     calls don't depend on each other and the prices fetch is the
     long-pole at ~6 s. ``asyncio.gather`` over ``asyncio.to_thread``
     brings warm cross-worker latency from ~11 s down to <6 s.
  3. Score + filter articles by relevance.
  4. For each surviving article timestamp, look up the most-recent
     price before the event and the next prices at +1h / +6h / +24h.
  5. Compute the realised σ of hourly log returns, then flag each
     event as ``attributable`` iff its 6h |move| exceeds 1.5σ.

Cache layers:
  - L1 (process-local dict, 15 min TTL) — exposed as ``_CACHE``.
  - L2 (Redis, 10 min TTL, payloads ≤ 128 KB) — shared across all
    gunicorn workers so a cold worker can warm-fill without re-hitting
    GDELT + CLOB.

so a second cluster call within ``CACHE_TTL_SECONDS=600`` hits warm.
Concurrency is bounded to :data:`CONCURRENCY` to avoid stampeding GDELT
+ the Gamma API.

get.summary: Paper-PnL backtest of the disagrees-jump reversion signal.
get.description:
Run the disagrees-jump paper-PnL backtest for one slug.

Pipeline (mirrors ``/terminal/jumps/{slug}``):

    1. Resolve market metadata + relevance terms.
    2. Fetch hourly prices (Polymarket CLOB) and multi-source news.
    3. Detect jumps from the price series; score articles for
       relevance; attach matched articles + aggregate sentiment to
       each jump.
    4. Hand the jump list + price series to
       :func:`simulate_disagrees_pnl`.

The agrees bucket is reported as a control — its mean should hover
near zero if the disagrees signal is genuine reversion.

get.summary: Detect price-series jumps and attach matching GDELT articles.
get.description:
For a Polymarket slug, return jumps (∆logit outliers) with matching news.

A jump fires when ``|Δlogit| ≥ mad_k × rolling_MAD`` OR
``|Δp| ≥ min_jump_pp / 100``. For each jump we look ±1h around the
timestamp in GDELT for articles relevant to the market question.
Jumps with no matching article are returned with ``explained=False``
— these are the rows worth a human read.

get.summary: Rank top-volume markets by news-sentiment / price-jump disagreement density.
get.description:
Top-25 markets where news-sentiment most often disagrees with price jumps.

Reads the top-50 markets by 24h volume from the same gamma listing the
homepage uses, fans out ``/terminal/jumps/{slug}`` calls with bounded
concurrency, and ranks by ``disagrees_pct = n_disagrees / n_jumps``
descending. Markets with fewer than ``min_jumps`` total jumps are
excluded — the ranking is noise-dominated when the denominator is tiny.

Cached for ``CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 600`` keyed on ``(days, min_jumps)``;
a warm hit returns in <2 ms.

get.summary: Hierarchical clustering of factors by Δlogit-return correlation.
get.description:
Cluster factors by return correlation and surface a leader per cluster.

Cached for ``CLUSTER_TTL_SECONDS`` (default 600 s) via the L1/L2 hybrid
TERMINAL_CACHE. The clustering work — pairwise correlation across up to
600 series + hierarchical cut — is ~300-600 ms p50, so amortising
across workers is a big perceived-latency win.

Warm-cache fast path: when invoked via HTTP and the lifespan prewarm
has stored a fresh default-payload (``theme=None, min_corr=0.5``) on
``app.state.warm_clusters``, we short-circuit before touching the L1/L2
cache. Cold-tail latency drops from ~3 s to <50 ms. See ``pfm.prewarm``.

get.summary: On-demand live signal + Kelly-scaled sizing for a single pair (bypasses the hourly batch).
get.description:
Compute the live signal for one curated pair on demand.

Cache policy:
  * ``force_refresh=True``: always recompute from Polymarket. If that
    fails, return 503.
  * Otherwise: if ``live_signals.json`` carries an entry whose
    ``as_of`` is within 30 minutes, return it (``data_source=
    "cached_batch"``). Else compute live; on compute failure fall back
    to any stale cached entry (``data_source="stale_fallback"``) with
    a warning.

Sizing:
  * ``kelly_fraction ≈ oos_sharpe / sqrt(252)`` clipped to
    ``[0, kelly_cap]``.
  * ``recommended_size_usd = kelly * suggested_allocation * bankroll``.
  * Both go to 0 when ``action ∉ {LONG/SHORT_SPREAD, OPEN_LONG/SHORT}``.

get.summary: Composite payload: top production alphas + watchlist + recent graveyard.
get.description:

* ``watchlist`` — top entries with tier ``B_VALIDATED`` (capped at 10).

post.description:
Fast pre-flight check for /fit.

Resolves factors, fetches equity returns + factor histories, joins them,
and reports per-factor coverage + joint n_obs. Does NOT run the regression
— meant to be called as the user is composing a /fit request so they can
see "you'll get 187 obs" before clicking Run.

Same cache as /fit, so a preview followed by a full /fit pays the upstream
cost once.

Smart factor picker — top-K factors most correlated with a ticker.

Computes |Pearson r| between the ticker's log returns and every
curated factor's Δlogit (Δlevel for non-probability factors), ranked
by |r|. Cached 1 h per (ticker, lookback_days, top_k, min_n_obs) at
two layers (process-local + Redis L2) with SETNX stampede protection
so 4 gunicorn workers share one cold scan instead of 4×.

Powers the frontend's "factors most likely to explain ``TICKER``"
panel in the Regression mode. Cold target: <60 s for 10 different
tickers in parallel. Warm target: <5 s for 10 same-ticker calls.

Test the logical-implication invariant ``A ⇒ B`` ⇒ ``P(A) ≤ P(B)``.

Returns per-date gaps and a verdict bucketing the count of violation dates.
Use it on logically-related markets (e.g. "BTC ≥ $150k" ⇒ "BTC ≥ $100k").
HAC-OLS regression of P_A on P_B (β interpretable as conditional sensitivity).
Per-date Fréchet-Hoeffding bounds on the joint ``P(A ∩ B)``.

Compare a live underlying (Binance daily klines) to a market-implied
YES-price for a price-target binary outcome.

Pulls a trailing window of daily OHLCV from Binance, estimates σ̂ via
Yang-Zhang OHLC, and computes a closed-form GBM probability for the
geometry chosen by the caller (terminal / one-touch up / one-touch down).
A block-bootstrap CI is returned alongside an "edge" against the
market price (when supplied).

See ``docs/strategies.md`` §6 for derivations and the
resolution-source-basis caveat (Polymarket BTC markets typically
settle on Coinbase / UMA, not Binance).

Engle-Granger 2-step cointegration test on a probability pair.

Returns the OLS hedge ratio, the ADF p-value on the residuals, and the
AR(1)-derived half-life. A short half-life with rejected ADF is the
signal a pairs trader looks for.

Engle-Granger cointegration test between a factor and a FRED macro series.

Pulls the factor's daily probability from Polymarket (via the chained
cache) and the FRED series via the auth-free ``fredgraph.csv`` endpoint,
aligns them on the UTC daily calendar, runs Engle-Granger 2-step, and
returns the OLS hedge ratio, ADF p-value, and the AR(1)-derived
half-life.

Args:
    body: validated request payload (factor id, FRED series id, window,
        optional transform applied to the FRED series before the test).

Returns:
    :class:`FredCointegrationResponse` with the cointegration verdict
    and a summary of the FRED series window (first/last/min/max).

Raises:
    HTTPException: 400 if ``start >= end`` or the factor id is unknown,
        502 if FRED returns an error, 422 on transform errors.

Walk-forward z-score pairs trade on the spread of two probability series.

First runs Engle-Granger to verify cointegration (informational only —
the backtest still runs even on non-cointegrated pairs, but the user
sees `cointegration_passed: false` and should weight the Sharpe down).
HAC-OLS regression of one event probability on N other events.

PCA-residual statistical arbitrage on a basket of related events.

Stack the chosen factor probability series into a matrix; subtract the
top-k principal components; the residuals are the *idiosyncratic*
inefficiency signals that pairs/basket stat-arb trades on. Each
residual gets a rolling z-score the user can use as an entry signal.
Includes a per-market Kelly-fraction estimate.

Calibrate OU dynamics on the cointegration spread + Bertram (2010)
optimal entry/exit z-bands.

Reports κ (mean-reversion speed), μ (long-run mean), σ_eq (equilibrium std),
half-life, and the analytically-optimal ``z_entry`` that maximises
expected PnL per unit time net of round-trip transaction cost.

Bivariate Granger causality between two event probability series.

For each lag in 1..max_lag, tests whether past values of one series
help predict the other. Used to identify the *leader* in event-pair
co-movement (long the follower, hedge with the leader).

Time-varying hedge ratio β_t via Kalman filter.

Returns the per-bar β̂_t and the *innovation* spread e_t = y_t − β_t·x_t,
which can be fed directly into the pairs-trading backtester for an
adaptive-hedge strategy. Smaller δ ⇒ slower, smoother β̂_t; larger
δ ⇒ more responsive (and noisier).

Hurst exponent (R/S) + Lo-MacKinlay variance-ratio test on a single
factor's probability series. Both quantify mean-reversion strength
model-free.

Auto-pipeline: scan catalog for cointegrated pairs, backtest each,
rank the leaderboard by Sharpe.

The single "press here for alpha" button. Restrict by ``theme`` for
speed; ``max_to_backtest`` caps how many of the top scanner hits get
the full pairs-trading backtest treatment.

Cross-pair structural-pattern analysis: PnL correlation matrix,
day-of-week effects per pair, pre-resolution regime shifts, k-means
clustering on pair signatures.

Use this on the OOS-validated leaderboard to surface portfolio-level
insights — pair independence, weekday seasonality, vol-explosion
near resolution, structural groupings.

Gradient-boosted regressor predicting next-bar Δspread from
engineered features (lag-z, rolling vol, autocorrelation, momentum,
long-window distance-from-mean). TimeSeriesSplit cross-validation.
Reports R², direction accuracy, information coefficient, beats-baseline,
feature importances, and a forward-looking last_prediction.

Use as a *complement* to the z-score state machine, not a replacement —
direction accuracy >55% across folds is the bar to clear before
integrating predictions into a live signal.

Hasbrouck (1995) Information Share — for two cointegrated price
series, decomposes the proportion of long-run price discovery each
venue contributes. The leader's IS is closer to 1; the follower's
closer to 0. Use to identify which venue (Kalshi vs Polymarket) drives
a cross-platform Fed-cut basis.

Hamilton (1989) Markov-switching variance model on the cointegration
spread. State 0 = tight mean-reversion (low σ, tradeable); state 1 =
broken (high σ, regime change risk). Returns smoothed P(state=1) per bar.

Practitioner use: if `current_regime_prob > 0.5`, suspend the pairs trade
and re-validate cointegration.

Closed-form Almgren-Chriss (2001) optimal execution trajectory. No
historical data needed — pure mathematical optimisation given the
target position and impact/risk parameters. Use to schedule entry of a
large pairs trade without telegraphing it to the market.

Hosking (1981) / López de Prado (2018 §5) fractional differentiation.

If ``d`` is None, finds the minimal d in (0, 1) that makes the series
stationary (ADF p < 0.05) — the López de Prado recipe to maximise
memory preservation while ensuring stationarity.

Bollerslev (1986) GARCH(1,1) — conditional volatility on Δ-series.

Fits μ, ω, α, β by MLE. Returns one-step-ahead σ forecast for live
vol-targeting and the per-bar conditional σ history for diagnostics.

Peng et al. (1994) Detrended Fluctuation Analysis — robust Hurst
exponent on the integrated/cumulative-sum series. Robust to
non-stationary trends. α<0.5 = mean-reverting; α≈0.5 = random walk;
α>0.5 = persistent; α>1 = non-stationary.

López de Prado (2018) Triple Barrier Method on a cointegration spread.

Adaptive vol-scaled exits: each trade has 3 barriers — profit target
(+pt·σ_local), stop loss (−sl·σ_local), time horizon (T bars). Exit
on first touch.

Gatev-Goetzmann-Rouwenhorst (2006) Distance Method.

Classical pairs-trading benchmark: form-period SSD on normalised
series, trade widest deviations during the trading period (no peeking).

Comprehensive robustness battery on a portfolio of pair trades.

Builds the portfolio PnL series via the same vol-targeted combiner used
by /strategies/portfolio, then runs 5 robustness tests:
1. Lo (2002) asymptotic Sharpe SE
2. Block bootstrap CI on Sharpe (Politis-Romano)
3. Sign-flip permutation null on Sharpe
4. Out-of-time (50/50 train/test) test
5. Deflated Sharpe Ratio (Bailey-Lopez de Prado, multiple-testing corrected)
Plus cost-sensitivity sweep.

Returns an overall verdict in {STRONG ALPHA, MARGINAL ALPHA,
WEAK / SUSPECT, NOISE / OVERFIT}.

Vol-targeted portfolio combiner. Aggregates the per-bar PnLs of N
pair-trading strategies into a single equity curve, weighted so each
leg contributes ``target_per_leg_vol`` annualised volatility.

Reports portfolio-level Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar, max DD, VaR/CVaR,
plus walk-forward OOS Sharpe distribution. Per-leg signal type can
be overridden per pair (``zscore`` or ``bollinger_15``).

Production-grade multi-event factor model.

Beyond basic event_model:
- Estimator choice: OLS / Ridge / Lasso / ElasticNet
- Logit transform option (handles [0,1] bounds)
- PCA pre-processing (collapse collinear factors)
- Residual diagnostics: Ljung-Box, Jarque-Bera, ARCH-LM, Durbin-Watson
- Cross-validated R² (TimeSeriesSplit) — true OOS, not in-sample
- Walk-forward β stability (mean ± std across folds)
- Bootstrap R² CI

Use Lasso when you have many candidate factors and want auto-selection.
Use Ridge when factors are collinear (VIF > 10).
Always check `r_squared_cv` (the trustworthy metric) — if it's far below
`r_squared_is`, the model is overfit.

Brown-Durbin-Evans CUSUM-OLS structural-break test on the
Engle-Granger spread. Detects level shifts / regime changes in the
cointegrating relationship — useful before deploying capital on a
pair: a recent break makes the historical β_hedge unreliable.

K-fold walk-forward backtest. Reports the *distribution* of test-fold
Sharpes — much more credible than a single train/test split. Stable =
min(test Sharpe) > 0 AND std(test Sharpe) < |mean|.

Stationary block-bootstrap (Politis-Romano 1994) CI on the Sharpe
of a pair's z-score backtest. CI excluding zero ⇒ Sharpe is statistically
distinguishable from random.

Permutation null distribution of the Sharpe ratio. Sign-flips the
spread's first differences; rebuilds; runs the same strategy; computes
Sharpe. ``p = P(null Sharpe ≥ real Sharpe)``. p < 0.05 ⇒ the real
Sharpe doesn't come from random fluctuations of the spread.

(default 180).

Performance hardening (2026-05-15):
  - Encoded with ``ORJSONResponse`` when ``orjson`` is installed
    (~3-5x faster than the stdlib ``json`` encode for the 135 KB blob).
    Falls back to ``JSONResponse`` transparently otherwise.
  - ``Cache-Control: public, max-age=2`` so the browser revalidates the
    body only every 2 s. The SSE stream still pushes every tick;
    ``/state`` is for the initial paint + manual refresh.

Uses the async loader so a stale-fallback rebuild (``top_arbs`` fans out
to ~10 sync HTTP requests, several seconds) runs in a worker thread
instead of blocking the uvicorn worker — measured: 0.8-3.6 s spikes
eliminated on the user-facing XHR.

get.description:
Expose the engine's live state for one symbol.

The frontend uses this to compute *model probability* for Polymarket
BTC/ETH strike markets: ``P(S_T > K) = 1 − Φ((ln(K/S) − μT) / (σ√T))``
with ``σ`` from the WS engine's realized vol (annualized) instead of a
hard-coded guess, and ``μ`` from the order-flow imbalance.

Returns ``{available: false}`` if the engine is off or the symbol hasn't
seen enough trades yet (needs ~30s of warmup). Unknown symbols (not in
the curated 10-pair list) return 404 instead of a misleading 200 with
null fields — caught by the QA agent.

post.description:
HTTP front for :func:`pfm.quant.regression_methods.fit_elastic_net`.

Validation rules (enforced before any network IO):

* ``alpha`` must be the literal string ``"auto"`` or a positive float.
  Pydantic accepts ``float`` for the union arm but allows zero/negative
  values — we re-check here so the user gets a clear 422 instead of
  sklearn's downstream ``ValueError``.
* ``l1_ratio`` is enforced ``in (0, 1]`` by the Pydantic field.
* ``cv_splits >= 2`` is enforced by the Pydantic field.
* Unknown factor ids → 404 with ``did_you_mean`` hints.

Returns the W11-57 dataclass fields renamed for the public API
(see module docstring for the mapping).

          "description": "Default. statsmodels OLS with cov_type='HAC'.",
          "supported": true,
          "params": ["lag"]
        },
        ...
      ]
    }

Methods whose backing stub still raises ``NotImplementedError`` are
advertised with ``supported: false`` and (where defined) a
``supported_after`` pointing at the wave that's expected to ship them.
Front-ends use this to grey out unsupported options in the method picker.

source.title: Source
source.description: 'live_signals' when the snapshot drove the trail; 'synthetic' otherwise.

max_to_backtest.title: Max To Backtest
max_to_backtest.description: Top-N cointegrated pairs to actually backtest (more is slow).

p_values.title: P Values
p_values.description: Marginal p-values to correct, each in [0, 1].

sharpe_naive.title: Sharpe Naive
sharpe_naive.description: Naive Sharpe: mean / std × √(252×24 / hold_hours). Zero when std=0 or n<2.

avg_win.title: Avg Win
avg_win.description: Mean return across winning trades (0 if none).

avg_loss.title: Avg Loss
avg_loss.description: Mean return across losing trades (0 if none).

max_drawdown.title: Max Drawdown
max_drawdown.description: Worst peak-to-trough drawdown on the cumulative-sum equity curve, expressed as a non-positive number.

total_return.title: Total Return
total_return.description: Sum of per-trade returns (terminal value of equity curve).

BacktestStats.title: BacktestStats
BacktestStats.description: Aggregate paper-PnL statistics for one bucket (disagrees or agrees).

criterion.title: Criterion
criterion.description: Selection criterion: in-sample R² adj, or chronological 80/20 OOS R².

filter_resolving.title: Filter Resolving
filter_resolving.description: Skip factors in resolution-collapse phase (extreme moves in last 14 days).

zscore.title: Zscore
zscore.description: Z-score each Δlogit so factors have unit variance during selection.

residualize_market.title: Residualize Market
residualize_market.description: Predict α (residual after SPY β) instead of raw return.

theme_composites.title: Theme Composites
theme_composites.description: Average factors within each theme into composites — reduces 30+ factors to ~7, fights overfitting.

max_per_theme.title: Max Per Theme
max_per_theme.description: Constrain stepwise to at most N factors per theme. Forces diversification.

BookLevel.title: BookLevel
BookLevel.description: One side of one level of the ladder, with running cumulative size.

BoundsRequest.title: BoundsRequest
BoundsRequest.description: Per-date Fréchet-Hoeffding bounds on the joint P(A∩B).

magnitude.title: Magnitude
magnitude.description: Bucket count divided by the rolling mean count.

Burst.title: Burst
Burst.description: A one-minute bucket flagged as a trade-count burst.

prefix.title: Prefix
prefix.description: Key prefix to clear within each matched pool. Passed straight to ``CachePool.clear(prefix=...)``. ``None`` or empty wipes the whole pool.

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: Restrict the operation to the pool whose ``CachePool.namespace`` matches exactly. ``None`` or empty means 'every discovered pool'.

CacheInvalidateRequest.title: CacheInvalidateRequest
CacheInvalidateRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /admin/cache/invalidate``.

At least one of ``prefix`` / ``namespace`` must be a non-empty string.
A body with both fields missing or empty fails Pydantic validation
with a 422 from FastAPI before the handler runs.

category.title: Category
category.description: fomc | cpi | nfp | election | crypto_* | ipo | debate

related_markets.title: Related Markets
related_markets.description: Factor IDs from factors.yml expected to react to this event.

days_until.title: Days Until
days_until.description: Calendar days from today (server clock, UTC).

CalendarItem.title: CalendarItem
CalendarItem.description: One row in the unified calendar feed.

implied_lambda.title: Implied Lambda
implied_lambda.description: Constant-hazard rate λ = -ln(1 - p) / T (per day).

CalendarLeg.title: CalendarLeg
CalendarLeg.description: One contract on the calendar surface.

surface.title: Surface
surface.description: All legs sorted by deadline ascending.

log_lambda_ratio.title: Log Lambda Ratio
log_lambda_ratio.description: ln(λ_far / λ_near) — positive ⇒ far deadline cheaper in hazard terms (i.e. expensive in price terms).

CalendarPairResponse.title: CalendarPairResponse
CalendarPairResponse.description: Term-structure surface for a calendar pair.

tagged_factor.title: Tagged Factor
tagged_factor.description: Factor id this item was tagged to (None = unmatched).

keyword_overlap.title: Keyword Overlap
keyword_overlap.description: Tokens shared with the factor's keywords.

delta_prob.title: Delta Prob
delta_prob.description: Polymarket probability change attributed to this item.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Why the chain landed at this confidence.

CausalLink.title: CausalLink
CausalLink.description: One news item with its full chain to ticker-level impacts.

ChannelRef.title: ChannelRef
ChannelRef.description:
Reference to a delivery channel.

Args:
    type: One of inapp / webhook / slack / discord.
    target: Channel-specific destination (URL for webhook/slack/discord,
        user_id for inapp).
    enabled: If False, this channel is skipped during fan-out.

ts_iso.title: Ts Iso
ts_iso.description: Representative timestamp (median of the member-jump timestamps).

n_articles.title: N Articles
n_articles.description: Total matched articles across all member jumps.

dominant_terms.title: Dominant Terms
dominant_terms.description: Top-K terms by frequency across the cluster (K=5).

representative_headline.title: Representative Headline
representative_headline.description: Single best headline — highest relevance × proximity inside the cluster — meant to be the human-readable label for the macro event.

Cluster.title: Cluster
Cluster.description: A group of ≥2 jumps tied together by time + keyword overlap.

sentiment_alignment.title: Sentiment Alignment
sentiment_alignment.description: Inherited from the underlying Jump: agrees / disagrees / neutral.

ClusterMember.title: ClusterMember
ClusterMember.description: One jump that's part of a cluster — slim reference back to the source.

dd.title: Dd
dd.description: Max drawdown of the combined equity curve (≤ 0).

jumps.title: Jumps
jumps.description: Per-slug largest signed delta_pp on this date, or null if the slug had no jump on this date.

shared_news_count.title: Shared News Count
shared_news_count.description: Number of distinct articles that appear in jumps of ≥2 slugs on this date. Identifier prefers URL with a SimHash fallback for near-duplicates across wires.

CommonDay.title: CommonDay
CommonDay.description: One UTC date on which ≥2 of the requested slugs had a jump.

CompareBar.title: CompareBar
CompareBar.description: One bar in a normalised history series (``t0 = 100``).

strategies.title: Strategies
strategies.description: Two-to-eight strategy specs to backtest side-by-side.

days.title: Days
days.description: Lookback window in calendar days. Each strategy is restricted to the last `days` of overlapping history.

CompareStats.title: CompareStats
CompareStats.description: Derived stats for a single leg over the requested window.

ok.title: Ok
ok.description: True iff the sample fetch succeeded.

sample_size.title: Sample Size
sample_size.description: Number of daily bars returned for the sample slug.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error class + message when ``ok`` is False.

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: The slug used for the connectivity probe.

CountdownMarket.title: CountdownMarket
CountdownMarket.description: One Polymarket market resolving inside the requested horizon.

lambda_range.title: Lambda Range
lambda_range.description: (min λ, max λ) across legs. ``null`` if no leg priced.

lambda_std.title: Lambda Std
lambda_std.description: Sample std of λ across legs; ``null`` for n<2.

lambda_ratio_front_back.title: Lambda Ratio Front Back
lambda_ratio_front_back.description: λ_front / λ_back — > 1 means front-leg hazard exceeds back.

trade_signal.title: Trade Signal
trade_signal.description: One of FLATTEN_CURVE | STEEPEN_CURVE | HOLD | INSUFFICIENT_DATA.

CuratedClusterDetail.title: CuratedClusterDetail
CuratedClusterDetail.description: Detail payload — adds the 90-day historical λ-ratio series.

lambda_range.title: Lambda Range
lambda_range.description: (min λ, max λ) across legs. ``null`` if no leg priced.

lambda_std.title: Lambda Std
lambda_std.description: Sample std of λ across legs; ``null`` for n<2.

lambda_ratio_front_back.title: Lambda Ratio Front Back
lambda_ratio_front_back.description: λ_front / λ_back — > 1 means front-leg hazard exceeds back.

trade_signal.title: Trade Signal
trade_signal.description: One of FLATTEN_CURVE | STEEPEN_CURVE | HOLD | INSUFFICIENT_DATA.

current_p.title: Current P
current_p.description: Latest mid; ``null`` when the upstream API failed.

implied_lambda.title: Implied Lambda
implied_lambda.description: Constant-hazard rate λ = -ln(1 - p) / T per day.

CuratedLeg.title: CuratedLeg
CuratedLeg.description: One contract on a curated calendar.

source_used.title: Source Used
source_used.description: Which upstream produced the returns series.

data_quality_note.title: Data Quality Note
data_quality_note.description: Human-readable warning when the synth fallback fired.

DeployableItem.title: DeployableItem
DeployableItem.description: One row in the deployable enumeration.

loss.title: Loss
loss.description: Loss function applied to errors before differencing.

count.title: Count
count.description: Number of alerts of this kind in the window.

examples.title: Examples
examples.description: Top-N example labels (slug or market title) — at most 3.

checked_at.title: Checked At
checked_at.description: UTC ISO8601 (Z) of when the digest was assembled.

whale_flow_pm.title: Whale Flow Pm
whale_flow_pm.description: Net USD flow from PM whale trades.

equity_flow.title: Equity Flow
equity_flow.description: Equity-side flow proxy: volume x sign(close - vwap).

divergence_strength.title: Divergence Strength
divergence_strength.description: 0 = aligned; 1 = max disagreement with high magnitudes.

factors.title: Factors
factors.description: Factor ids / slugs from the catalog (at least one).

alpha.title: Alpha
alpha.description: Overall regularisation strength. Either the literal string ``"auto"`` for CV selection, or a positive float.

l1_ratio.title: L1 Ratio
l1_ratio.description: L1 mixing fraction in ``(0, 1]``. 1.0 → pure LASSO.

ts_iso.title: Ts Iso
ts_iso.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the article.

attributable.title: Attributable
attributable.description: True iff |6h move| > 1.5 sigma of hourly returns.

matched_terms.title: Matched Terms
matched_terms.description: Anchor/topic terms that contributed to the relevance score.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: One GDELT article paired with the price reaction in its window.

target_id.title: Target Id
target_id.description: Factor id of the target probability series.

factor_ids.title: Factor Ids
factor_ids.description: Factor ids of the regressors (between 1 and 20).

delta_r_squared.title: Delta R Squared
delta_r_squared.description: R²(full) - R²(without this factor). Positive = factor helps. Values <= 0.01 typically indicate the factor is redundant; values >= 0.05 indicate a meaningful contributor.

share_of_explained_r_squared.title: Share Of Explained R Squared
share_of_explained_r_squared.description: delta_r_squared / sum(delta_r_squared across factors), clipped to [0, 1]. Useful as a 100% share-of-voice bar.

FactorContributionOut.title: FactorContributionOut
FactorContributionOut.description:
Leave-one-out R² impact for a single factor.

Re-fits the model without this factor and reports how much R² the full
model wins by keeping it. ``delta_r_squared`` close to zero means the
factor is redundant given the others; large positive values mean it is
doing real explanatory work.

n_obs_available.title: N Obs Available
n_obs_available.description: Total raw observations the source returned for this factor (before the [start, end] window filter).

n_obs_in_window.title: N Obs In Window
n_obs_in_window.description: Observations remaining after the [start, end] window filter — the value the inner-join with equity will use.

FactorCoverageOut.title: FactorCoverageOut
FactorCoverageOut.description:
Per-factor coverage stats for /fit/preview.

The ``n_obs_available`` / ``n_obs_in_window`` split lets the user spot a
factor that exists upstream but barely overlaps with the requested
window — the most common cause of an inner-join collapse on /fit.

FactorIndexRow.title: FactorIndexRow
FactorIndexRow.description:
One factor row in the palette dump.

Fields are deliberately one-letter to keep the payload small — there
are 1090 factors so any per-row overhead matters.

FactorList.title: FactorList
FactorList.description:
Paginated factor list.

The ``total`` / ``limit`` / ``offset`` / ``next_offset`` fields were
added once the catalog grew past 1 000 entries; the legacy ``factors``
array is preserved so existing clients ``r.json()["factors"]`` keep
working. ``next_offset`` is ``None`` when there are no more pages.

FactorMetadataOut.title: FactorMetadataOut
FactorMetadataOut.description:
Per-factor metadata returned alongside the regression fit.

Lets the client see, at a glance, which factors are probabilities (so
Δlogit applies) vs level series (Δ first-difference), and how many raw
observations each factor contributed before the inner-join with the
equity returns. Helps diagnose why a window has fewer obs than expected.

FactorTracePoint.title: FactorTracePoint
FactorTracePoint.description: One observation of a factor's underlying probability path.

anchor.title: Anchor
anchor.description: The anchor factor slug echoed back.

related.title: Related
related.description: Top-N related factors sorted by |ρ| desc. Empty when no other factor has ≥20 overlapping daily observations with the anchor.

FillCost.title: FillCost
FillCost.description: Fill cost in cents (notional) for a buy and sell of the given USD size.

FitPreviewRequest.title: FitPreviewRequest
FitPreviewRequest.description: Fast pre-flight check for /fit — same shape, no regression run.

joint_n_obs.title: Joint N Obs
joint_n_obs.description: Inner-joined sample size after aligning equity returns with all factors' Δlogit. This is the actual ``n_obs`` /fit would use.

joint_window_obs.title: Joint Window Obs
joint_window_obs.description: Same value as ``joint_n_obs`` exposed under the canonical naming used by the rigour-pack docs.

factor_coverage_map.title: Factor Coverage Map
factor_coverage_map.description: Per-factor coverage keyed by factor_id for O(1) lookup from the frontend. Same values as ``factor_coverage`` (which is a list) — pick whichever shape the client prefers.

predicted_window_n_obs.title: Predicted Window N Obs
predicted_window_n_obs.description: Predicted ``n_obs`` /fit will see after inner-joining equity + every factor's Δlogit. Lets the user know BEFORE clicking Run how many days will survive the join.

FitPreviewResponse.title: FitPreviewResponse
FitPreviewResponse.description:
What a /fit on these inputs WOULD see, without running the regression.

Used by the UI to show users a live "obs available" counter before they
click Run, and to surface "this factor only covers 23% of your window"
warnings up front rather than after a 4-second fit.

hac_lag.title: Hac Lag
hac_lag.description: Newey-West HAC bandwidth. None ⇒ Andrews (1991) automatic bandwidth. Must be < n_obs after factor alignment, else 422.

lag.title: Lag
lag.description: Shift factors by k days for predictive regression.

pca_components.title: Pca Components
pca_components.description: If set, reduce factors via PCA.

summary.title: Summary
summary.description: One-line plain-English readout: 'K factors fit with R²=X; N significant at p<0.05; M high-VIF warning'.

verdict.title: Verdict
verdict.description: Single-word fit-quality flag: well_specified | weak_fit | collinear | underpowered | borderline.

top_significant.title: Top Significant
top_significant.description: Factor ids with p < 0.05, sorted by |t-stat| descending.

auto_pruned.title: Auto Pruned
auto_pruned.description: Factor ids dropped by the auto-collinearity pruner. Empty when prune_collinear=False or when no factor exceeded VIF >= 5.

rolling_betas_ci.title: Rolling Betas Ci
rolling_betas_ci.description: Per-factor 60-day rolling β with HAC 95% CIs. Skipped (empty dict) when n_obs < 90; downsampled to ≤200 points per factor.

oos_r_squared.title: Oos R Squared
oos_r_squared.description: Walk-forward 5-fold OOS R² (median across folds). Returns an OosRSquaredSkipped block (with ``skipped_reason``) when n_obs < 100 — never silently null.

residual_annotations.title: Residual Annotations
residual_annotations.description: Up to 5 top-|residual| dates with per-factor Δlogit·β contribution attribution. Useful for event-driven outliers.

factor_correlation_matrix.title: Factor Correlation Matrix
factor_correlation_matrix.description: Pearson r between Δlogit series of factors (capped at 30 factors). Helps spot collinearity beyond VIF.

pca_summary.description: PCA explained-variance + top loadings on the design matrix. Skipped when k=1 (one factor, no decomposition possible).

next_step_hint.title: Next Step Hint
next_step_hint.description: One-sentence actionable suggestion derived from ``verdict``. Frontend displays this verbatim under the verdict pill.

live_signal.description: Forward-looking signal: predicted next-period return using the latest Δlogit row + the fitted β. Null when the design matrix has no usable last row.

pseudo_backtest.description: Daily-rebalanced replay of sign(predicted) over the fit window with a flat transaction cost. Skipped when n_obs < 30.

factor_contributions.title: Factor Contributions
factor_contributions.description: Per-factor leave-one-out R² impact, sorted descending. Null when the fit has only one factor (LOO is undefined).

overfit_risk_flags.title: Overfit Risk Flags
overfit_risk_flags.description: Structured warnings about overfit risk — n/k ratio, clipping saturation, sign inconsistency, theme mismatch. UI shows these as colored chips above the regression table.

multitest_hint.description: Session-level multiple-testing context: tests run this session and the resulting α/N threshold.

regime_changes.title: Regime Changes
regime_changes.description: Per-factor structural-break findings (Chow-style split-sample z-test on betas). Empty when nothing trips p<0.10 or n_obs < 60.

d.title: D
d.description: Differencing exponent. If None, finds the minimal d that makes series stationary (ADF p<0.05).

fred_series.title: Fred Series
fred_series.description: FRED series id; one of DFF/DGS2/DGS10/CPIAUCSL/UNRATE/VIXCLS.

transform.title: Transform
transform.description: Transform applied to the FRED series before the test.

FredCointegrationRequest.title: FredCointegrationRequest
FredCointegrationRequest.description:
Request body for ``/strategies/fred-cointegration``.

Tests cointegration between a Polymarket factor's probability series
and a curated FRED macro series fetched via the auth-free
``fredgraph.csv`` endpoint.

last_updated.title: Last Updated
last_updated.description: ISO date of most recent observation we know about (best-effort)

citation.title: Citation
citation.description: Public FRED page URL for the series

FredSeriesMetadata.title: FredSeriesMetadata
FredSeriesMetadata.description: Pydantic schema describing a single FRED series.

tone.title: Tone
tone.description: GDELT tone in [-10, +10]; 0 if not provided.

matched_terms.title: Matched Terms
matched_terms.description: Anchor/topic terms that contributed to the relevance score.

nlp_sentiment_score.title: Nlp Sentiment Score
nlp_sentiment_score.description: Hybrid VADER + finance-lex sentiment, signed [-1,+1]. Distinct from `tone` which is GDELT-native.

nlp_sentiment_label.title: Nlp Sentiment Label
nlp_sentiment_label.description: Bucketed label: positive / negative / neutral (±0.15 deadband).

GraveyardEntry.title: GraveyardEntry
GraveyardEntry.description:
Schema for a single graveyard row.

The model is permissive on extra fields so future waves can extend the
registry without breaking deployed clients (Pydantic v2 default).

vol_reduction_ratio.title: Vol Reduction Ratio
vol_reduction_ratio.description: std(hedged daily PnL) / std(unhedged daily PnL); <1 means hedge worked.

HistoricalRatioPoint.title: HistoricalRatioPoint
HistoricalRatioPoint.description: One day on the historical λ-ratio time-series.

HomepageMover.title: HomepageMover
HomepageMover.description: One row in gainers / losers / most-active.

antecedent_id.title: Antecedent Id
antecedent_id.description: Factor id of the *more specific* event (A in A⇒B).

consequent_id.title: Consequent Id
consequent_id.description: Factor id of the *broader* event (B in A⇒B).

tolerance.title: Tolerance
tolerance.description: Ignore gaps with P(A)-P(B) ≤ tolerance (default ≈ typical Polymarket bid-ask half-spread).

ImplicationRequest.title: ImplicationRequest
ImplicationRequest.description: Test the logical implication ``A ⇒ B`` ⇒ ``P(A) ≤ P(B)``.

handle.title: Handle
handle.description: Opaque handle to retrieve the portfolio downstream.

total_cost_basis.title: Total Cost Basis
total_cost_basis.description: Sum of provided cost_basis values. Rows without a cost_basis contribute 0 to this total — see warnings.

hold_days.title: Hold Days
hold_days.description: Optional time stop in bars (force-close after N days).

side.title: Side
side.description: Restrict trades to long-spread, short-spread, or both.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Strategy selector. ``pair`` requires a cointegrated peer in the alpha-hunter sweep; ``rolling_z`` and ``bollinger`` are self-referential fallbacks; ``auto`` tries pair first.

bollinger_k.title: Bollinger K
bollinger_k.description: Bollinger-band width in sigmas (only used when mode='bollinger').

InlineBacktestRequest.title: InlineBacktestRequest
InlineBacktestRequest.description:
User-supplied thresholds for the inline backtester.

Defaults match the Terminal preset: a 20-bar rolling window, ±2σ
entry, ±0.5σ exit, ±4σ stop, and no time stop. ``side`` filters
the trade tape to long-only or short-only spreads — useful when
the analyst has a directional view on the dominant peer.

``mode`` selects the strategy. ``auto`` tries the cointegrated
pair-trade first and silently falls back to ``rolling_z`` when the
slug has no peer in the alpha-hunter sweep.

mode_used.title: Mode Used
mode_used.description: The strategy that actually ran (pair / rolling_z / bollinger).

peer_slug.title: Peer Slug
peer_slug.description: Dominant cointegrated peer (only set in pair mode).

beta_hedge.title: Beta Hedge
beta_hedge.description: OLS hedge ratio reused from the sweep (only set in pair mode).

trade_pnls.title: Trade Pnls
trade_pnls.description: Per-trade PnL series (chronological, dollar PnL on a unit spread).

note.title: Note
note.description: Human-readable annotation (e.g. why a fallback mode was used).

price_before.title: Price Before
price_before.description: Price at t-1 (just before the jump).

explained.title: Explained
explained.description: True iff ≥1 GDELT article with relevance≥floor in the window.

news_sentiment_score.title: News Sentiment Score
news_sentiment_score.description: Mean sentiment across matched articles, in [-1, +1].

sentiment_alignment.title: Sentiment Alignment
sentiment_alignment.description: Does aggregate news sentiment match the price jump direction? 'agrees' = same sign; 'disagrees' = opposite (interesting!); 'neutral' = either side too close to 0 to call.

Jump.title: Jump
Jump.description: One detected price-jump with its (possibly empty) news context.

seconds_from_jump.title: Seconds From Jump
seconds_from_jump.description: Article ts minus jump ts, in seconds. Negative = before.

JumpArticle.title: JumpArticle
JumpArticle.description: One GDELT article matched to a jump's [-2h, +1h] window.

slugs.title: Slugs
slugs.description: Echoed slugs in the order received.

common_days.title: Common Days
common_days.description: UTC dates with ≥2 slugs having a jump, sorted ascending.

jumps_by_slug.title: Jumps By Slug
jumps_by_slug.description: Full per-slug jump list for every requested slug.

side.title: Side
side.description: BUY / SELL if reported by data-api.

LargeTrade.title: LargeTrade
LargeTrade.description: One recent large trade across the whale cohort.

LeaderInfo.title: LeaderInfo
LeaderInfo.description:
Leader factor for a cluster, with the lag at which it best
anticipates the cluster-mean move.

LeaderboardItem.title: LeaderboardItem
LeaderboardItem.description: Slim per-strategy view used by the leaderboard grid.

LeaderboardResponse.title: LeaderboardResponse
LeaderboardResponse.description:
Wrapper returned by ``GET /alpha-hub/leaderboard`` (slim default mode).

The ``full=true`` query param switches the handler to a richer payload
that's NOT modelled here (returned via :class:`JSONResponse` so every
raw-catalog field is preserved verbatim — ``data_quality_warning``,
``a_name``/``b_name``, ``sharpe_ci_lo``, ``rationale``, etc.). That
response also carries a ``meta`` block with top-level summary counts.

last_value.title: Last Value
last_value.description: Most recent price/level observed for the factor.

LeaderboardRow.title: LeaderboardRow
LeaderboardRow.description: One row of the theme leaderboard.

LivePanelResponse.title: LivePanelResponse
LivePanelResponse.description: Composite payload for the alpha-hub landing card.

predicted_return.title: Predicted Return
predicted_return.description: Model-implied next-period return on the latest Δlogit row. Sign tells you direction; magnitude is in the same units as the regression target (log-return when return_type='log').

std_err.title: Std Err
std_err.description: OLS prediction standard error at the latest design row. Reflects parameter uncertainty (β's HAC SE) and intercept noise; does NOT include unforecastable next-period shock.

latest_date.title: Latest Date
latest_date.description: Date of the design-matrix row used to compute the signal.

latest_factor_logits.title: Latest Factor Logits
latest_factor_logits.description: Per-factor Δlogit on ``latest_date`` so the caller can see which markets moved into the prediction.

low_confidence.title: Low Confidence
low_confidence.description: True when verdict is 'weak_fit' or 'underpowered' — the UI should show a 'illustration only' caveat.

LiveSignalOut.title: LiveSignalOut
LiveSignalOut.description:
Forward-looking prediction from the just-fit model.

Computed as ``predicted = α + Σᵢ βᵢ · Δlogit_today_i`` using the last
available Δlogit per factor (final non-null row of the design matrix).
Carries the OLS prediction standard error and the 95 % CI so the UI can
colour the number and surface the uncertainty. Set to ``None`` when the
fit window has no usable last row (e.g. every factor went stale).

as_of.title: As Of
as_of.description: UTC ISO-8601 of the signal observation.

computed_at.title: Computed At
computed_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 of when this response was assembled.

LiveSignalResponse.title: LiveSignalResponse
LiveSignalResponse.description: On-demand live-signal payload for a single pair (see contract).

MacroOverlaySeries.title: MacroOverlaySeries
MacroOverlaySeries.description: A single series in the overlay response.

fair_price_at_resolution.title: Fair Price At Resolution
fair_price_at_resolution.description: 0 or 1 — the expected payoff per YES contract assuming the current market probability becomes the realised outcome at T+0.

expected_payoff_if_held.title: Expected Payoff If Held
expected_payoff_if_held.description: Expected $ payoff per $1 of YES bought now and held to resolution: current_p * 1 - 1*current_p = 0 in expectation under no edge; the field reports current_p directly so the UI can render YES/NO PnL skeleton.

MarketCountdown.title: MarketCountdown
MarketCountdown.description: Real-time countdown for one specific market.

equity_beta.title: Equity Beta
equity_beta.description: Estimated equity-equivalent β contribution per $1 long.

cvar_95_usd.title: Cvar 95 Usd
cvar_95_usd.description: Mean PnL conditional on PnL ≤ 5th pct.

percentiles.title: Percentiles
percentiles.description: Selected percentiles of the simulated PnL distribution.

bh_q_threshold.title: Bh Q Threshold
bh_q_threshold.description: Per-test Bonferroni-style threshold α/N at α=0.05.

message.title: Message
message.description: Human-readable advisory. UI displays verbatim under the p-value column.

MultitestHint.title: MultitestHint
MultitestHint.description:
Session-level multiple-testing context for the caller.

Driven by the ``X-Session-Test-Count`` request header (default 1). Lets
the UI render a "your effective threshold is α/N" advisory next to the
p-values so the user doesn't fool themselves after running 30 fits.

Notes
-----
The hint uses Bonferroni-style ``α/N`` for the readout because it's the
quantity a quant intuitively expects when comparing a single p to a
family of tests. The proper Benjamini-Hochberg q-thresholds depend on
the rank order of *all* p-values in the family, which the server has no
way to know across calls — the caller can use ``/quant/multitest/bh``
for the exact procedure.

q.title: Q
q.description: The original query string, echoed back.

since.title: Since

score.title: Score
score.description: Token-Jaccard similarity between query and title, in [0,1].

matched_factors.title: Matched Factors
matched_factors.description: Factor slugs whose ``name`` shares ≥1 non-stop token with the article title. Empty when ``factors=false`` or no factor matches.

NewsSearchResult.title: NewsSearchResult
NewsSearchResult.description: One scored news article matching the query.

nested.title: Nested
nested.description: If True (default) use Clark-West nested-model correction.

value.title: Value
value.description: Median test-fold R² across the walk-forward folds.

per_fold.title: Per Fold
per_fold.description: Per-fold test R² values, in chronological order.

OosRSquared.title: OosRSquared
OosRSquared.description:
Walk-forward out-of-sample R² block emitted by the enriched ``/fit``.

Reports the median (across folds) of the test-fold R², plus train/test
sizes and fold count. Useful for spotting in-sample overfit: when the
headline ``r_squared`` is high but ``oos_r_squared.value`` collapses,
the model is memorising rather than generalising.

value.title: Value
value.description: Always null in the skipped state.

skipped_reason.title: Skipped Reason
skipped_reason.description: Human-readable reason the walk-forward was not run, e.g. 'n_obs=32 < min_n_for_walk_forward=100'.

OosRSquaredSkipped.title: OosRSquaredSkipped
OosRSquaredSkipped.description:
Explicit "OOS skipped" state — replaces the silent ``null`` shape.

Returned in place of :class:`OosRSquared` when the helper opted out
(e.g. n_obs below the walk-forward floor). Lets the UI render a clear
"skipped — needs ≥100 obs" badge instead of silently hiding the field.

Backward compatibility: clients that read ``oos_r_squared.value`` still
work because the union of OosRSquared | OosRSquaredSkipped | None
keeps ``None`` as a fallback if even the skipped block can't be built.

transaction_cost_sigma.title: Transaction Cost Sigma
transaction_cost_sigma.description: Round-trip cost in multiples of σ_eq. Polymarket round-trip ≈ 1-3¢ on a typical 0.1-0.3 σ spread → 0.05-0.30.

code.title: Code
code.description: Stable enum: low_dof | moderate_dof | high_clipping | sign_inconsistent | theme_mismatch.

OverfitRiskFlag.title: OverfitRiskFlag
OverfitRiskFlag.description:
One structured guardrail for the user about fit reliability.

``level`` lets the UI pick a colour (high=red, medium=yellow, low=blue);
``code`` is a stable machine-readable enum so dashboards can filter.
The ``message`` is human-readable plain English ready for display.

source.title: Source
source.description: Whether the slugs for this bucket came from the persisted live-validated map or the hardcoded fallback.

as_of.title: As Of
as_of.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last successful refresh (None if never).

source.title: Source
source.description: ``live`` → all buckets from disk, ``fallback`` → all hardcoded, ``mixed`` → some of each.

buckets.title: Buckets
buckets.description: Bucket -> slug list currently used by /indices/pm-vix.

n_dead_replaced.title: N Dead Replaced
n_dead_replaced.description: Buckets where one or more dead slugs were swapped in.

buckets.title: Buckets
buckets.description: Bucket -> active slug list after the refresh.

cache_age_seconds.title: Cache Age Seconds
cache_age_seconds.description: Seconds since this snapshot was computed. ``0`` for a freshly computed payload, larger when served from the prewarmed cache.

u.title: U
u.description: Relative URL the front-end should route to.

oos_fraction.title: Oos Fraction
oos_fraction.description: Fraction of bars held out as out-of-sample tail. Sharpe IS / Sharpe OOS lets you spot overfitting.

max_hold_bars.title: Max Hold Bars
max_hold_bars.description: Half-life-aware time stop: force-close any position held longer than this many bars. Defaults to None (no time stop).

top_loadings.title: Top Loadings
top_loadings.description: For each component index (0-based) the factor → loading dict (top 5 factors by |loading|). Lets the UI label PCs as "mostly Fed factors" or "mix of A and B".

PcaSummary.title: PcaSummary
PcaSummary.description:
Quick PCA on the design matrix — first ``n_components`` of variance.

Helpful for users who pile in many correlated factors and want a one-glance
view of how many independent dimensions actually drive the regressors.

PermutationRequest.title: PermutationRequest
PermutationRequest.description:
Standalone permutation-test request — same shape as FitRequest minus
the optional analyses. Returns a `PermutationResult`.

delta_logit_assumed.title: Delta Logit Assumed
delta_logit_assumed.description: Optional hypothetical Δlogit. If set, response includes expected_return_pct = β · Δlogit · 100 for each ticker.

PriceChangePctRule.title: PriceChangePctRule
PriceChangePctRule.description: Fires when |Δprice| / price over a rolling window exceeds pct_abs.

PriceCrossRule.title: PriceCrossRule
PriceCrossRule.description:
Fires when contract price crosses a threshold (with hysteresis).

Hysteresis prevents flap: once fired on ">", the rule must dip below
threshold - hysteresis before it can re-arm.

equity_curve.title: Equity Curve
equity_curve.description: Daily {date, predicted, actual, position, pnl, equity}.

annualized_sharpe.title: Annualized Sharpe
annualized_sharpe.description: sqrt(252) · mean(pnl) / std(pnl). 0 when std is 0.

hit_rate.title: Hit Rate
hit_rate.description: Fraction of days where sign(predicted) == sign(actual).

n_trades.title: N Trades
n_trades.description: Number of position changes (sign flips or flat→position).

transaction_cost_bp.title: Transaction Cost Bp
transaction_cost_bp.description: Per-trade cost charged in basis points (default 5).

note.title: Note
note.default: Naive in-sample replay. Ignores factor latency, fees beyond the flat per-trade cost, slippage, and capacity. Illustrative only — confirm with out-of-sample data before acting.

PseudoBacktestOut.title: PseudoBacktestOut
PseudoBacktestOut.description:
In-sample, naive-trader replay of the fitted model.

For each day in the fit window, take ``position = sign(predicted)``,
earn ``actual × position`` minus a per-trade transaction cost, and
compound. Reports the resulting equity curve and four headline stats.

This is deliberately naive — no out-of-sample split, no slippage, no
capacity model, no factor latency. It's an "if you'd traded the signal
in this window" sanity check. Use ``oos_r_squared`` for generalisation
evidence.

checked_at.title: Checked At
checked_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the audit.

rejection_breakdown.title: Rejection Breakdown
rejection_breakdown.description: Count per rejection reason (T77 hard-reject taxonomy).

quarterly_sharpes.title: Quarterly Sharpes
quarterly_sharpes.description: Per-quarter Sharpe ratios in chronological order.

threshold.title: Threshold
threshold.description: Minimum per-quarter Sharpe to count as 'positive'.

min_n_for_ranking.title: Min N For Ranking
min_n_for_ranking.description: Minimum overlapping observations required to enter the primary ranking by R². Factors below the threshold are returned but sorted after the well-sampled ones (and after errors).

breakpoint_date.title: Breakpoint Date
breakpoint_date.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the split point. The 'post' half starts at this date inclusive.

sign_flipped.title: Sign Flipped
sign_flipped.description: True when sign(pre_beta) != sign(post_beta) AND both magnitudes exceed 0.01 (avoids near-zero noise flips).

p_value.title: P Value
p_value.description: Two-sided p for the per-factor beta difference, smallest across the candidate breakpoints.

RegimeChangeOut.title: RegimeChangeOut
RegimeChangeOut.description:
One per-factor structural-break finding from :mod:`pfm.regression_regime`.

Reported only when the per-factor split-sample test trips the p-value
threshold (default 0.10). Empty list when nothing was detected.

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: Polymarket slug / source-id for the related factor.

p_value.title: P Value
p_value.description: Two-sided p-value for ρ=0 under H0.

RelatedFactor.title: RelatedFactor
RelatedFactor.description: One related factor, ranked by |ρ| against the anchor's price series.

cache_age_seconds.title: Cache Age Seconds
cache_age_seconds.description: Seconds since this scenario was computed. ``0`` for a freshly computed payload, larger when served from the 24h cache.

factor_attribution.title: Factor Attribution
factor_attribution.description: Per-factor contribution Δlogit·β on that date. The factor with the largest |contribution| is the one most likely to explain the miss.

top_factor.title: Top Factor
top_factor.description: Id of the factor with the largest |contribution| on this date.

news_links.title: News Links
news_links.description: External-search URLs (TICKER + date) the user can click to find the news that may explain this residual. Plain links — the frontend deep-links them under each annotation.

ResidualAnnotation.title: ResidualAnnotation
ResidualAnnotation.description:
Top-|e_t| residual outlier with the factor that drove it most.

Surfaces the few dates where the model fit worst, alongside which
factor's contribution Δlogit·β was the largest (in absolute value)
on that date. Lets the user spot event-driven outliers (e.g. a Fed
decision day) without scanning the full residual series.

ResolvedMarketSummary.title: ResolvedMarketSummary
ResolvedMarketSummary.description: One row of the ``/markets`` list response.

candidate_factor_ids.title: Candidate Factor Ids
candidate_factor_ids.description: Pool of factor ids to consider. If null, the candidate pool is derived from the ``pool`` query param (default: top-100 by 24h volume; opt-in ``pool=all`` for the full ~1360 sweep).

pool_used.title: Pool Used
pool_used.description: Which candidate pool produced the iterated factor list: ``top_volume`` / ``all`` / ``theme`` / ``explicit``. The ``top_volume`` mode also surfaces the source via the trailing label (e.g. ``top_volume:terminal_homepage``).

n_candidates_evaluated.title: N Candidates Evaluated
n_candidates_evaluated.description: Number of factor ids actually iterated by the stepwise scan.

RiskBudgetAllocation.title: RiskBudgetAllocation
RiskBudgetAllocation.description: A single line item in the risk-budget table.

n_trials_searched.title: N Trials Searched
n_trials_searched.description: Multiple-testing correction: how many strategies were evaluated before settling on this portfolio. Conservative default 100. Affects deflated Sharpe ratio.

RobustValidationRequest.title: RobustValidationRequest
RobustValidationRequest.description:
Validates the alpha of a portfolio of pair trades against a battery
of robustness tests.

Robustness.title: Robustness
Robustness.description: Minimal robustness envelope for the deployable card.

RollingBetaCiPoint.title: RollingBetaCiPoint
RollingBetaCiPoint.description:
Single point on a per-factor rolling-β series with HAC 95% CIs.

Used by the enriched ``/fit`` response (``rolling_betas_ci``) so the UI
can plot a per-factor β(t) line with shaded confidence band without
re-fitting on the client. Each window's ``ci_lo`` / ``ci_hi`` are the
Newey-West 95% bounds computed at the same lag as the headline fit.

pub_date.title: Pub Date

relevance_score.title: Relevance Score
relevance_score.description: Question-relevance in [0, 1]; only set for slug-matched feeds.

matched_terms.title: Matched Terms

RssHeadline.description: One parsed headline from a single RSS source.

topics.title: Topics
topics.description: Question topic terms (content words) used for relevance scoring.

relevance_min.title: Relevance Min

factor_ids.title: Factor Ids
factor_ids.description: Explicit factor ids to scan; overrides theme when given.

implied_lambda.title: Implied Lambda
implied_lambda.description: Constant-hazard rate λ = -ln(1 - p) / T (per day).

volume_24h.description: 24h notional volume from the gamma listing.

n_disagrees.title: N Disagrees
n_disagrees.description: Jumps where news sentiment disagrees with the price-jump direction.

SentimentLeaderboardRow.description: One row in the leaderboard: a single market summarised.

lead_lag_days.description: Lag k in [-7, +7] at which the correlation peaks. Positive means news leads price by k days; 0 means contemporaneous; negative means price leads news.

degraded_mode.description: True when no usable tone signal was retrieved from the upstream GDELT API (e.g. throttling, network error, or no tone returned).

symbol.description: Binance trading pair (uppercase). Default uses Binance spot — note that Polymarket BTC markets typically settle on Coinbase / UMA-disputed indexes, so a small basis is expected.

strike.title: Strike
strike.description: Target price K. For 'terminal' geometry: settles 1 if S_T ≥ K. For 'one_touch_up': 1 if M_T = max S_t ≥ K. For 'one_touch_down': 1 if min S_t ≤ K.

market_prob.title: Market Prob
market_prob.description: Current market YES-price (0..1). When provided, the response includes 'edge' = market_prob − model_prob and edge_significant_95.

drift_annual.title: Drift Annual
drift_annual.description: Annualised drift μ. Default 0 (risk-neutral, no carry for unfunded BTC spot). Override with the perp funding rate if you prefer a carry adjustment.

interval.title: Interval
interval.description: Binance kline interval. Sub-daily intervals (e.g. 5m) give a tighter, more responsive σ̂ for short-dated markets.

vol_window_bars.title: Vol Window Bars
vol_window_bars.description: Trailing window of bars (matching ``interval``) used for σ̂ estimation. 90 daily bars ≈ 3 months; 1000 5-min bars ≈ 3.5 days.

SpotVsImpliedRequest.title: SpotVsImpliedRequest
SpotVsImpliedRequest.description:
Compare a live underlying (Binance daily klines) to a market-implied
YES-price for a price-target binary outcome.

StrategyEntry.title: StrategyEntry
StrategyEntry.description: One row in the catalog enumeration.

StrategyPreset.title: StrategyPreset
StrategyPreset.description: Curated example for a given strategy, tuned from real catalog hits.

StrategyResult.title: StrategyResult
StrategyResult.description: Per-strategy diagnostics for the compare panel.

side.title: Side
side.description: Direction filter: long-spread, short-spread, or both.

StrategySpec.title: StrategySpec
StrategySpec.description:
One strategy in the comparison set.

Mirrors :class:`pfm.terminal_inline_backtest.InlineBacktestRequest`
plus the slug-to-test, so each strategy is fully self-describing.

SuggestForTickerItem.title: SuggestForTickerItem
SuggestForTickerItem.description: One row of the smart-picker response.

lookback_days.title: Lookback Days
lookback_days.description: Trailing window in calendar days for the correlation scan.

top_k.title: Top K
top_k.description: Number of top factors to return, ranked by |Pearson r|.

min_n_obs.title: Min N Obs
min_n_obs.description: Skip factors that have fewer than this many overlapping obs.

SuggestForTickerRequest.title: SuggestForTickerRequest
SuggestForTickerRequest.description:
Request body for the smart-factor-picker endpoint.

Computes |Pearson r| between the ticker's log returns and each curated
factor's Δlogit, then returns the top ``top_k`` ranked by |r|. Cached
for 1 h per (ticker, lookback_days) so the UI's "suggest factors" panel
is snappy on repeat loads.

n_factors_scanned.title: N Factors Scanned
n_factors_scanned.description: Total candidate factors considered (after coverage filter).

n_factors_skipped.title: N Factors Skipped
n_factors_skipped.description: Factors skipped due to no data or n_obs below ``min_n_obs``.

factor_ids.title: Factor Ids
factor_ids.description: Optional restriction to a subset of factor ids. When None, the full factors.yml catalog is used.

mean_tone.title: Mean Tone
mean_tone.description: Average article tone; 0 if all missing.

anchors.title: Anchors
anchors.description: Question anchor terms (entities) used for relevance scoring.

topics.title: Topics
topics.description: Question topic terms (content words) used for relevance scoring.

relevance_min.title: Relevance Min
relevance_min.description: Score threshold below which articles are dropped.

anchors.description: Question anchor terms (entities) used for matching.

topics.title: Topics
topics.description: Question topic terms (content words) used for matching.

relevance_min.title: Relevance Min
relevance_min.description: Score threshold below which items are dropped.

degraded_mode.title: Degraded Mode
degraded_mode.description: True when every upstream news source (Reddit + HN) failed; the payload still validates (empty ``items``) so the UI renders a graceful 'news unavailable' state instead of a 502.

TerminalSearchIndexChunkResponse.title: TerminalSearchIndexChunkResponse
TerminalSearchIndexChunkResponse.description:
One slice of the factor catalogue for the lazy palette path.

The frontend calls ``chunk=0`` on ⌘-K open to paint the palette
immediately, then prefetches subsequent chunks idle. Strategies /
pages / actions are tiny and ride along on every chunk so the
palette can search them without a second request.

informed_flow_alert.title: Informed Flow Alert
informed_flow_alert.description: True if any 5-min window crossed the informed-flow threshold.

factors.title: Factors
factors.description: Factors in the theme sorted desc by ``vol_7d``. Factors with ``n_obs < 30`` are excluded unless ``include_dead=true``.

ThemesResponse.title: ThemesResponse

expected_return_pct.title: Expected Return Pct
expected_return_pct.description: Forecast log-return in percent (β * Δlogit * 100). ``None`` when the chain cannot produce a return (no Δprob or no β).

TickerImpact.title: TickerImpact
TickerImpact.description: Per-ticker stock-return forecast given the inferred Δlogit.

dominant_topic.title: Dominant Topic

expected_impact_pct.title: Expected Impact Pct
expected_impact_pct.description: max-|expected_return_pct| across affected tickers, signed in the direction of the Δlogit move.

TopTrade.title: TopTrade

entry_target_cents.title: Entry Target Cents

TradeTick.description: One classified trade in the tape.

url.title: Url

n_sources.title: N Sources

hours_since.title: Hours Since

checked_at.title: Checked At

VenueMarket.title: VenueMarket
VenueMarket.description: One market hit from any of the four venues.

VolumeSpikeRule.title: VolumeSpikeRule
VolumeSpikeRule.description: Fires when today's volume is more than n_sigma above lookback mean.

alert_z.title: Alert Z

last_active_iso.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of last activity, if known.

n_trades_24h.title: N Trades 24H

strategy_returns_matrix.title: Strategy Returns Matrix

benchmark_returns.description: Length-T benchmark return series; pass zeros for raw excess-vs-zero.
